published_prior_art.cite: Kunsch1989BlockJackknife and ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife supply the block-jackknife and recomputation precedents; the exact ratio is a calibration reduction.
ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife supplies the Neyman-Jackknife framework and cycle/Newey--West construction, but no verified schedule-uniform sqrt(M/n) expected-inflation theorem.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt_ego_cluster_polynomial_clt and exp_bipartite_minimax_design_v1 concern different interference minimax problems and do not define R_n^star(M) over recompute laws and schedule-uniform proxies.

published_prior_art.cite: ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife treats fixed recomputation pairs; HarshawMiddletonSavje2026OptimizedVariance optimizes quadratic conservative bounds; neither gives an all-update-law, all-observable-proxy inflation lower bound.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Develop the converse handle into an explicit nuisance-rich schedule family that preserves the constant-direct-effect and arc-variance conditions while remaining indistinguishable through the proxy sigma-field.
Prove a matching decomposition of the padded-block risk into separate M/L and L/n terms before optimizing L, so the source of the sqrt(M/n) balance is transparent and reusable beyond cycles.
Add a corollary or numerical worked cycle instance comparing the padded block with a disconnected update law, making the all-rule scope and the practical tuning implication concrete.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 while treating the explicit nuisance-rich indistinguishability construction for the all-rule converse as the decisive open kernel.
